sprint: Traveller Decline Reset — My Projects No Longer Leaks, the Ceremony Re-Offers Correctly
task: A direct, explicit design question that itself pointed at the real gap, plus a concrete reproduction: 'but should these 3 flags should not be removed as soon we start the traveller journey? try this flow. start as traveller become creator than reload the page on challenge screen go back and choose to move forward as traveller. with this flow i have 2 observations. 1. from studio when you click home, you can see previous projects. 2 magic card generation ceremony not showing up.' Traced to js/gatewaySequence.js's runVideoSequence's 'Continue as a Traveller' decline handler and js/magicCard.js's shouldOfferAwakening(), finding two real, compounding gaps in exactly the reported sequence. Gap 1: the decline handler already cleared CreatorProjectStore's own catalog (an earlier fix), but left the SEPARATE ProjectManager live-session key (vihustudio-session -- the actual in-progress project, not the catalog listing) completely untouched, so the very next autosave silently re-populated 'My Projects' with the just-declined Creator's own project. Fixed by also calling the pre-existing ProjectManager.discardSession() at the same decline point. Gap 2: shouldOfferAwakening() gated on 'has any card EVER been claimed on this device' instead of 'is one currently active right now', permanently exhausting the ceremony offer for the rest of the device's life the instant the first Creator ever claimed -- compounded by the device-wide awakeningOffered flag never resetting either. Fixed by rewriting the gate to !getActive() && !_readFlags().awakeningOffered, and having the decline handler also call MagicCard.setActive(null) + MagicCard.setFlags({awakeningOffered:false}). Deliberately NOT wiped: the real Magic Card record itself (vihu-magic-cards) -- on a shared device this may be a sibling's own real, recoverable card, so only the active pointer/flags are cleared, never the record. Verified end-to-end via a comprehensive Playwright reproduction driving the real UI throughout (a real Publish claim ceremony to completion, a real page reload, the real Returning Creator sky-recognition challenge, a real decline via its choice screen, then a second real Publish as the now-reset Traveller): confirmed My Projects genuinely empties, no restore modal appears, Creation Flow Screen 1 is reached cleanly, the underlying card record survives untouched (cardCountStillOnDevice:1), and the second Publish genuinely re-offers the Awakening ceremony. A separate, targeted regression check confirmed a SUCCESSFULLY-verified Returning Creator still correctly has shouldOfferAwakening() return false afterward -- the rewritten gate does not wrongly re-offer the ceremony to an already-recognized Creator. Full regression across the entire existing Gateway/sky-recognition-challenge/companion/My-Projects/pointer-guard Playwright suite passes unchanged; a handful of individual runs showed pre-existing, already-disclosed test-script staleness unrelated to this fix (a decoy-vs-real-card coin-flip in one script's own retry loop; several older scripts' fixed wait timeouts written before earlier voice-duration-driven-timing fixes shipped, all re-verified passing once widened to match current, correct behaviour) -- none caused by this fix. Both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- this fix touches zero Builder files) pass unchanged, 30/30. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/gatewaySequence.js and js/magicCard.js.
